1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# scripts/benchmark/benchmark — operator-facing benchmark driver.
3	#
4	# Replaces `fiz-bench sweep` / `fiz-bench matrix` execution. Pure shell:
5	# yq + jq + sha256sum + docker. No Go bridge, no Python.
6	#
7	# Bead fizeau-fe87a3ac: skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode +
8	# preflight rebuild gate. The cell execution loop (resume, retry, signal
9	# handling, concurrency, budget) lands in a follow-up bead.
10	#
11	# Operator contract:
12	#   ./benchmark profiles
13	#   ./benchmark bench-sets
14	#   ./benchmark harness-adapters
15	#   ./benchmark task-executors
16	#   ./benchmark validate                            # placeholder; see PR C
17	#   ./benchmark preflight                           # rebuild harbor-runner if sha drifted
18	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] --plan
19	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...]   # run path; PR A4
20	#
21	# Rules:
22	#   1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls/builds, no
23	#      benchmark-results/ creation. Prints one matrix line per cell.
24	#   2. --profile and --bench-set are both required for --plan and run.
25	#   3. preflight is the only command that touches Docker.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
30	PROFILES_DIR="${PROFILES_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/profiles}"
31	BENCH_SETS_DIR="${BENCH_SETS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/bench-sets}"
32	HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/harness-adapters}"
33	TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/task-executors}"
34	HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/build-harbor-runner.sh"
35	HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG:-fizeau-harbor-runner:latest}"
36	DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT:-bench/results/fiz-tools-v1}"
37	BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/external/terminal-bench-2"
38	BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS:-4}"
39	BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE:-1}"
40	FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}"
41	BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS:-30}"
42	
43	export SCRIPT_DIR \
44	  PROFILES_DIR \
45	  BENCH_SETS_DIR \
46	  HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR \
47	  TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR \
48	  HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT \
49	  HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG \
50	  DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT \
51	  BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT \
52	  BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS \
53	  BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE \
54	  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR \
55	  BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS

57	die() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
58	warn() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
59	
60	require_tool() {
61	  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
62	}
63	
64	# in_flight_state_dir returns the host-specific state directory.
65	in_flight_state_dir() {
66	  local hostname
67	  hostname="$(hostname)"
68	  printf '%s/%s\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${hostname}"
69	}
70	
71	# in_flight_lock_path returns the lock path for a concurrency group.
72	in_flight_lock_path() {
73	  local group="$1"
74	  printf '%s/locks/%s.lock\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${group}"
75	}
76	
77	# in_flight_json_path returns the path to the in-flight state JSON file.
78	in_flight_json_path() {
79	  printf '%s/in-flight.json\n' "$(in_flight_state_dir)"
80	}
81	
82	# acquire_group_lock acquires an flock for a concurrency group, blocking if needed.
83	# Returns 0 on success, prints the lock fd to stdout.
84	acquire_group_lock() {
85	  local group="$1"
86	  local lock_path
87	  lock_path="$(in_flight_lock_path "${group}")"
88	  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${lock_path}")"
89	  local lock_fd
90	  lock_fd=200
91	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>'${lock_path}'"
92	  flock "${lock_fd}" || die "failed to acquire lock for concurrency-group=${group}"
93	  printf '%d\n' "${lock_fd}"
94	}
95	
96	# release_group_lock releases an flock acquired by acquire_group_lock.
97	release_group_lock() {
98	  local lock_fd="$1"
99	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>&-" || true
100	}
101	
102	# export_cell_worker_functions exports the shell functions that the
103	# per-cell setsid worker needs after bash -c hands execution off to a
104	# fresh session leader.
105	export_cell_worker_functions() {
106	  export -f \
107	    die \
108	    warn \
109	    in_flight_state_dir \
110	    in_flight_lock_path \
111	    in_flight_json_path \
112	    release_group_lock \
113	    register_inflight \
114	    unregister_inflight \
115	    interrupt_inflight_cells \
116	    is_terminal_status \
117	    resolve_adapter_for_harness \

121	    write_sweep_metadata \
122	    compose_env_redacted \
123	    is_transient_stderr \
124	    run_executor_with_retry \
125	    resolve_tasks_dir \
126	    inspect_existing_cells \
127	    compute_cell_cost \
128	    budget_path \
129	    init_budget \
130	    append_budget_cell \
131	    budget_is_halted \

135	}

137	# launch_cell_worker starts a cell in a fresh session so the worker's PID
138	# doubles as the process-group leader for interrupt reaping.
139	launch_cell_worker() {
140	  local lock_fd="$1"
141	  shift
142	  setsid bash -c '
143	    budget_or_run_cell "$@"
144	    status=$?
145	    release_group_lock "${!#}"
146	    exit "${status}"
147	  ' bash "$@" "${lock_fd}" &
148	}

150	# register_inflight registers a cell as in-flight in the state file.
151	# Reads current entries, adds this cell, removes dead PIDs, writes back.
152	register_inflight() {
153	  local cell_id="$1" cell_dir="$2"
154	  local state_dir
155	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
156	  mkdir -p "${state_dir}"
157	  local json_path
158	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
159	
160	  local current_json="{\"cells\":[]}"
161	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] && current_json="$(cat "${json_path}")" || true
162	
163	  # Prune dead PIDs by filtering in bash
164	  local live_cells=()
165	  local cell_json pid
166	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
167	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
168	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
169	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
170	      live_cells+=("${cell_json}")
171	    fi
172	  done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' <<<"${current_json}")
173	
174	  # Rebuild with live cells
175	  current_json="$(printf '%s\n' "${live_cells[@]}" | jq -s '{cells:.}')"
176	
177	  # Add this cell
178	  current_json="$(jq \
179	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
180	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
181	    --argjson pid "${BASHPID}" \
182	    '.cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, cell_dir:$cell_dir, pid:$pid}]' \
183	    <<<"${current_json}")"
184	
185	  printf '%s\n' "${current_json}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
186	  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
187	}
188	
189	
190	# unregister_inflight removes a cell from the in-flight state file.
191	unregister_inflight() {
192	  local cell_id="$1"
193	  local json_path
194	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
195	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
196	
197	  local updated
198	  updated="$(jq \
199	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
200	    '.cells |= map(select(.cell_id != $cell_id))' \
201	    "${json_path}")"
202	
203	  printf '%s\n' "${updated}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
204	  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
205	}

207	# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to mark in-flight
208	# cells as interrupted and clean them up from the JSON file.
209	interrupt_inflight_cells() {
210	  local json_path
211	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
212	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
213	
214	  local cells
215	  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0
216	
217	  local cell_id cell_dir pid
218	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
219	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
220	    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
221	    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
222	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
223	
224	    # Try to kill the cell's process group
225	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
226	      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || kill -TERM "${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
227	    fi
228	
229	    # Try to docker stop any harbor-runner containers in this cell
230	    if [[ -d "${cell_dir}" ]]; then
231	      # The container ID is typically embedded in result.json or we can search by cell_dir
232	      # For now, we attempt a generic docker stop on containers that might be for this cell
233	      docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau-cell=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
234	        | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
235	    fi
236	  done <<<"${cells}"
237	}

242	  ./benchmark profiles

246	  ./benchmark validate
247	  ./benchmark preflight

249	              [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--max-cost-usd X]

252	Flags (run mode):
253	  --profile P[,P2,...]   Comma-separated profile IDs (required)
254	  --bench-set B[,B2,...] Comma-separated bench-set IDs (required)
255	  --plan                 Validate + print matrix; pure, no side effects
256	  --out DIR              Output root (default: ${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}); ignored under --plan
257	  --force-rerun          Always mint reps fresh cells; ignore terminal report.json

306	# list_adapter_dir prints one "<name>\t<summary>" line per executable file.
307	# Files without a `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2 print "(no summary)" so the
308	# omission is visible. CONTRACT.md and other non-executables are skipped.
309	list_adapter_dir() {
310	  local dir="$1"
311	  [[ -d "${dir}" ]] || { warn "directory missing: ${dir}"; return 0; }
312	  local f name summary
313	  local rows=()
314	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
315	  for f in "${dir}"/*; do
316	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
317	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
318	    summary="$(sed -n '2{/^# SUMMARY:[[:space:]]*/{s///;p;};q;}' "${f}")"
319	    [[ -n "${summary}" ]] || summary="(no summary)"
320	    rows+=("$(printf '%s\t%s' "${name}" "${summary}")")
321	  done
322	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
323	  if [[ ${#rows[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
324	    warn "no adapters found in ${dir}"
325	    return 0
326	  fi
327	  printf '%s\n' "${rows[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
328	}

354	bench_set_json() {

359	# resolve_tasks emits one task id per line for a bench-set.
360	# Handles three shapes:
361	#   tasks: [id, id, ...]         inline list
362	#   tasks: all                   defer to harness dataset; emit "*all*"
363	#   tasks_from: <path>           load tasks[].id from a sibling YAML
364	# tasks_from and inline tasks merge in deterministic order with duplicates
365	# removed on first occurrence.
366	resolve_tasks() {
367	  local bs_file="$1"
368	  local bs_json tasks_kind tasks_from
369	  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "${bs_file}")"
370	
371	  tasks_kind="$(jq -r '
372	    if (.tasks // null) == null then "none"
373	    elif (.tasks | type) == "array" then "list"
374	    elif (.tasks | type) == "string" then ("string:" + .tasks)
375	    else "other"
376	    end' <<<"${bs_json}")"
377	
378	  tasks_from="$(jq -r '.tasks_from // ""' <<<"${bs_json}")"
379	
380	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "string:all" ]]; then
381	    [[ -z "${tasks_from}" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_file}: tasks: all cannot be combined with tasks_from"
382	    # Defer to the harness dataset. The plan path emits a sentinel so
383	    # operators see the contract; the run path (PR A4) expands at execution.
384	    printf '*all*\n'
385	    return 0
386	  fi
387	
388	  local -A seen_tasks=()
389	  local emitted=0
390	  local task resolved
391	
392	  emit_task() {
393	    local task="$1"
394	    [[ -n "${task}" ]] || return 0
395	    if [[ -z "${seen_tasks["${task}"]+x}" ]]; then
396	      seen_tasks["${task}"]=1
397	      printf '%s\n' "${task}"
398	      emitted=1
399	    fi
400	  }
401	
402	  if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" ]]; then
403	    resolved="$(cd "$(dirname "${bs_file}")" && cd "$(dirname "${tasks_from}")" && pwd)/$(basename "${tasks_from}")"
404	    [[ -f "${resolved}" ]] || die "tasks_from path not found: ${tasks_from} (resolved to ${resolved})"
405	    while IFS= read -r task; do
406	      emit_task "${task}"
407	    done < <(
408	      yq -o=json '.' "${resolved}" \
409	        | jq -r '
410	            .tasks[]?
411	            | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
412	          '
413	    )
414	  fi
415	
416	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
417	    while IFS= read -r task; do
418	      emit_task "${task}"
419	    done < <(jq -r '
420	      .tasks[] | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
421	    ' <<<"${bs_json}")
422	  elif [[ "${tasks_kind}" != "none" ]]; then
423	    die "bench-set ${bs_file}: unsupported tasks shape"
424	  fi
425	
426	  if [[ ${emitted} -eq 0 ]]; then
427	    if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" || "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
428	      die "bench-set ${bs_file}: resolved no tasks"
429	    fi

523	  local concurrency_json="$1"

593	# validate_harness_adapters checks adapter install/command JSON contracts for
594	# every executable in scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/.
595	validate_harness_adapters() {
596	  local tmpdir="$1" synthetic_profile="$2"
597	  local artifact="${tmpdir}/agent-artifact"
598	  : >"${artifact}"
599	
600	  local f name install_out command_out install_err command_err install_status command_status
601	  local files=()
602	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
603	  for f in "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"/*; do
604	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
605	    files+=("${f}")
606	  done
607	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
608	  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no harness adapters found in ${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"
609	
610	  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
611	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
612	    install_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.install.err"
613	    command_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.command.err"
614	
615	    set +e
616	    install_out="$("${f}" install "${artifact}" 2>"${install_err}")"
617	    install_status=$?
618	    set -e
619	    if [[ ${install_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
620	      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install failed: $(<"${install_err}")"
621	    fi
622	    jq -e '
623	      type == "object"
624	      and (.install_command | type == "string" and length > 0)
625	      and (.artifact_source | type == "string" and length > 0)
626	      and (.binary_path | type == "string")
627	      and (has("harbor_plugin"))
628	    ' <<<"${install_out}" >/dev/null \
629	      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install output is malformed"
630	
631	    set +e
632	    command_out="$(printf '%s' "${synthetic_profile}" | "${f}" command 2>"${command_err}")"
633	    command_status=$?
634	    set -e
635	    if [[ ${command_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
636	      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command failed: $(<"${command_err}")"
637	    fi
638	    jq -e '
639	      type == "object"
640	      and (.command | type == "array" and length > 0)
641	      and all(.command[]?; type == "string")
642	      and (.env | type == "object")
643	      and all((.env // {}) | to_entries[]?; (.value | type == "string"))
644	      and (.secret_env_keys | type == "array")
645	      and ((.secret_env_keys | unique | length) == (.secret_env_keys | length))
646	      and (.env as $env | all(.secret_env_keys[]?; $env[.] != null))
647	    ' <<<"${command_out}" >/dev/null \
648	      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command output is malformed"
649	  done
650	}

694	# print_plan emits one line per (profile, task, rep) cell. Pure: no writes.
695	print_plan() {
696	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" reps_override="${3:-}"
697	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
698	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"
699	
700	  local profile_id bs_id bs_file framework dataset reps task_executor tasks
701	  local rep i n
702	
703	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
704	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
705	    resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}" >/dev/null  # existence check
706	    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
707	      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
708	      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
709	      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
710	      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
711	      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
712	        reps="${reps_override}"
713	      else
714	        reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
715	      fi
716	      task_executor="$(resolve_bench_set_task_executor "${bs_file}")"
717	      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
718	      i=0
719	      while IFS= read -r task; do
720	        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
721	        i=$((i + 1))
722	        for ((rep = 1; rep <= reps; rep++)); do
723	          n=$((rep))
724	          printf 'profile=%s\tbench_set=%s\tframework=%s\tdataset=%s\ttask=%s\trep=%d/%d\ttask_executor=%s\n' \
725	            "${profile_id}" "${bs_id}" "${framework}" "${dataset}" \
726	            "${task}" "${n}" "${reps}" "${task_executor}"
727	        done
728	      done <<<"${tasks}"
729	      [[ "${i}" -gt 0 ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: no tasks resolved"
730	    done
731	  done
732	}

734	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
735	# preflight: rebuild harbor-runner if image-content-sha drifted
736	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
737	
738	# compute_harbor_source_sha mirrors build-harbor-runner.sh's algorithm: a
739	# sha256 over harbor_adapters/* + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.
740	# Kept in sync by re-computing the same inputs; the two scripts MUST agree.
741	compute_harbor_source_sha() {
742	  local adapters_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
743	  local agent_file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"
744	  local dockerfile="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
745	  [[ -d "${adapters_dir}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_adapters/ missing (${adapters_dir})"
746	  [[ -f "${agent_file}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_agent.py missing (${agent_file})"
747	  [[ -f "${dockerfile}" ]] || die "preflight: Dockerfile.harbor-runner missing (${dockerfile})"
748	  {
749	    LC_ALL=C find "${adapters_dir}" -type f \
750	      | LC_ALL=C sort \
751	      | while IFS= read -r f; do
752	          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#"${SCRIPT_DIR}/"}"
753	        done
754	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${agent_file}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
755	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${dockerfile}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
756	  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
757	}
758	
759	# cached_harbor_image_sha prints the image-content-sha label of the cached
760	# image, or empty if the image is absent / the label is missing.
761	cached_harbor_image_sha() {
762	  local tag="$1"
763	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { printf ''; return 0; }
764	  docker image inspect --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "image-content-sha" }}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || printf ''
765	}
766	
767	cmd_preflight() {
768	  require_tool sha256sum
769	
770	  local failed=0
771	  local source_sha cached_sha
772	  local state_dir host_state_dir lock_dir probe
773	  local build_output build_rc
774	
775	  printf 'preflight checklist:\n'
776	
777	  check_tool() {
778	    local tool="$1"
779	    if command -v "${tool}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
780	      printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${tool}" "$(command -v "${tool}")"
781	      return 0
782	    fi
783	    printf '  [ ] %s (missing)\n' "${tool}"
784	    failed=1
785	    return 1
786	  }
787	
788	  check_writable_dir() {
789	    local label="$1" dir="$2" tmp
790	    if mkdir -p "${dir}" 2>/dev/null; then
791	      tmp="${dir}/.preflight.$$"
792	      if : >"${tmp}" 2>/dev/null; then
793	        rm -f "${tmp}"
794	        printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
795	        return 0
796	      fi
797	    fi
798	    printf '  [ ] %s (%s not writable)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
799	    failed=1
800	    return 1
801	  }
802	
803	  check_tool yq
804	  check_tool jq
805	  check_tool docker
806	  check_tool setsid
807	  check_tool flock
808	
809	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
810	  lock_dir="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/locks"
811	  check_writable_dir "benchmark state dir" "${state_dir}"
812	  check_writable_dir "benchmark lock dir" "${lock_dir}"
813	
814	  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
815	  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
816	  printf '  [x] harbor source fingerprint (%s)\n' "${source_sha}"
817	  printf '  [x] cached harbor image fingerprint (%s)\n' "${cached_sha:-<none>}"
818	
819	  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
820	    printf '  [x] harbor-runner image up to date (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
821	  else
822	    if [[ ! -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]]; then
823	      printf '  [ ] harbor-runner build script executable (%s missing or not executable)\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
824	      failed=1
825	    else
826	      printf '  [*] building harbor-runner image via %s\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
827	      set +e
828	      build_output="$(IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" 2>&1)"
829	      build_rc=$?
830	      set -e
831	      printf '%s\n' "${build_output}"
832	      if [[ ${build_rc} -eq 0 ]]; then
833	        printf '  [x] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
834	      else
835	        printf '  [ ] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
836	        failed=1
837	      fi
838	    fi
839	  fi
840	
841	  if [[ ${failed} -ne 0 ]]; then
842	    printf 'preflight: one or more checks failed\n' >&2
843	    return 1
844	  fi
845	
846	  printf 'preflight: all checks passed\n'
847	}

852	
853	# Terminal final_status values that satisfy resume coverage. Non-terminal
854	# statuses (running, transient_exhausted, missing_result) do not count as
855	# coverage; --retry-invalid surfaces invalid_class != "" or orphan state.
856	is_terminal_status() {
857	  case "$1" in
858	    completed|pass|fail|timeout|invalid|completed_no_grade) return 0 ;;
859	    *) return 1 ;;
860	  esac
861	}

863	# Map the profile's `harness:` field to a harness-adapter file name.
864	# `none` (or absent) means native fiz.
865	resolve_adapter_for_harness() {
866	  case "${1:-}" in
867	    ""|none) printf 'fiz\n' ;;
868	    anthropic) printf 'claude\n' ;;
869	    *)       printf '%s\n' "$1" ;;
870	  esac
871	}

873	# Mint a fresh cell-id: UTC timestamp + 4 random hex chars.
874	new_cell_id() {
875	  local ts rand
876	  ts="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
877	  rand="$(LC_ALL=C tr -dc 'a-f0-9' </dev/urandom 2>/dev/null | head -c 4 || true)"
878	  if [[ -z "${rand}" || ${#rand} -lt 4 ]]; then
879	    rand="$(printf '%04x' $((RANDOM & 0xFFFF)))"
880	  fi
881	  printf '%s-%s\n' "${ts}" "${rand}"
882	}

884	# task_executor_version: sha256 of the harbor task-executor script. The
885	# task-executor is shell, so the script bytes are the version of record.
886	task_executor_version() {
887	  local f="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
888	  if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then
889	    sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}'
890	  else
891	    printf 'unknown\n'
892	  fi
893	}

895	# harbor_runner_image_digest: docker image Id for fizeau-harbor-runner. When
896	# docker is absent (CI / dry-run), fall back to the source-content sha so the
897	# sweep manifest is still well-defined.
898	harbor_runner_image_digest() {
899	  # Test/operator override wins so suites can pin the recorded digest.
900	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
901	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE}"
902	    return 0
903	  fi
904	  local tag="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
905	  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
906	    local d
907	    d="$(docker image inspect --format '{{.Id}}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
908	    if [[ -n "${d}" ]]; then
909	      printf '%s\n' "${d}"
910	      return 0
911	    fi
912	  fi
913	  # No docker (or image absent). Derive from the source-content sha used by
914	  # preflight.
915	  if compute_harbor_source_sha >/dev/null 2>&1; then
916	    printf 'src:%s\n' "$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
917	  else
918	    printf 'unknown\n'
919	  fi
920	}

922	# Write <out>/sweep.json with the executor + image identifiers. Called once
923	# per ./benchmark invocation; each cell's report.json embeds the same values.
924	write_sweep_metadata() {
925	  local out="$1" te_ver="$2" hd="$3"
926	  mkdir -p "${out}"
927	  local started
928	  started="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
929	  jq -n \
930	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
931	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
932	    --arg started "${started}" \
933	    '{task_executor_version:$te_ver, harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd, started_at:$started}' \
934	    >"${out}/sweep.json.tmp"
935	  mv "${out}/sweep.json.tmp" "${out}/sweep.json"
936	}

938	# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
939	# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
940	# ignored.
941	compose_env_redacted() {
942	  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
943	  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
944	    . as $env
945	    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
946	        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
947	  ' <<<"${env_json}"
948	}

950	# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
951	# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
952	is_transient_stderr() {
953	  local f="$1"
954	  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
955	  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
956	}

958	# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
959	# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
960	# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
961	run_executor_with_retry() {
962	  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
963	  local stderr_file
964	  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
965	  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
966	  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
967	  local attempt=1
968	  local status=0
969	  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
970	  : >"${retry_log}"
971	  while :; do
972	    : >"${stderr_file}"
973	    set +e
974	    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
975	    status=$?
976	    set -e
977	    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
978	    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
979	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
980	      return 0
981	    fi
982	    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
983	    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
984	      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
985	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
986	      return "${status}"
987	    fi
988	    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
989	    sleep "${backoff}"
990	    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
991	    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
992	  done
993	}

1009	# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
1010	# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
1011	# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
1012	inspect_existing_cells() {
1013	  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
1014	  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
1015	  : >"${terminal_out}"
1016	  : >"${invalid_out}"
1017	  : >"${orphan_out}"
1018	  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
1019	  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
1020	  local cell
1021	  shopt -s nullglob
1022	  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
1023	    local cdir="${cell%/}"
1024	    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
1025	    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
1026	    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
1027	      local pid status inv
1028	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1029	      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1030	      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1031	      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1032	      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
1033	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
1034	        continue
1035	      fi
1036	      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
1037	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
1038	      fi
1039	      continue
1040	    fi
1041	    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
1042	      local pid
1043	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1044	      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1045	      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
1046	    fi
1047	  done
1048	  shopt -u nullglob
1049	}

1051	# compute_cell_cost prints the cost_usd_at_run_time for a single cell. Uses
1052	# profile.pricing × token streams pulled from the cell's result.json. Token
1053	# field names follow the canonical report-level naming
1054	# (input_tokens / output_tokens / cached_input_tokens) and are also probed
1055	# under the harbor totals naming (total_prompt_tokens / total_completion_tokens
1056	# / total_cached_input_tokens) so harness-native token reporters land in the
1057	# same accumulator.
1058	#
1059	# When profile.pricing is all zero (or unset) and the executor wrote an
1060	# explicit cost_usd / total_cost_usd in result.json, the explicit value is
1061	# honored — that lets harnesses that observe API-reported cost directly
1062	# participate in budget enforcement.
1063	compute_cell_cost() {
1064	  local result_json="${1:-}" profile_json="${2:-}"
1065	  [[ -n "${result_json}" ]] || result_json='{}'
1066	  [[ -n "${profile_json}" ]] || profile_json='{}'
1067	  jq -nr \
1068	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1069	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1070	    '
1071	      def num(v): if (v == null) then 0 else (v | tonumber? // 0) end;
1072	      ($profile.pricing // {}) as $p
1073	      | num($p.input_usd_per_mtok)         as $pi
1074	      | num($p.output_usd_per_mtok)        as $po
1075	      | num($p.cached_input_usd_per_mtok)  as $pc
1076	      | num($result.input_tokens
1077	            // $result.total_prompt_tokens
1078	            // $result.n_input_tokens
1079	            // 0)                          as $ti
1080	      | num($result.output_tokens
1081	            // $result.total_completion_tokens
1082	            // $result.n_output_tokens
1083	            // 0)                          as $to
1084	      | num($result.cached_input_tokens
1085	            // $result.total_cached_input_tokens
1086	            // $result.n_cached_input_tokens
1087	            // 0)                          as $tc
1088	      | num($result.cost_usd // $result.total_cost_usd // 0) as $reported
1089	      | (($ti * $pi) + ($to * $po) + ($tc * $pc)) / 1000000  as $priced
1090	      | (if $priced > 0 then $priced
1091	         elif ($pi + $po + $pc) == 0 then $reported
1092	         else 0
1093	         end)
1094	      | tostring
1095	    '
1096	}

1098	# budget_path prints the canonical budget.json path under <out>.
1099	budget_path() {
1100	  printf '%s\n' "$1/budget.json"
1101	}

1103	# init_budget writes <out>/budget.json with the configured cap and an empty
1104	# accumulator. Idempotent: preserves existing cells/total when the cap
1105	# matches (resume case).
1106	init_budget() {
1107	  local out="$1" cap="$2"
1108	  local bp
1109	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1110	  if [[ -f "${bp}" ]]; then
1111	    local prev_cap
1112	    prev_cap="$(jq -r '.max_cost_usd // ""' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1113	    if [[ "${prev_cap}" == "${cap}" ]]; then
1114	      return 0
1115	    fi
1116	  fi
1117	  jq -n \
1118	    --argjson cap "${cap}" \
1119	    '{max_cost_usd:$cap, total_cost_usd:0, halted:false, cells:[]}' \
1120	    >"${bp}.tmp"
1121	  mv "${bp}.tmp" "${bp}"
1122	}

1124	# append_budget_cell folds one closed cell's cost into budget.json and prints
1125	# the post-update total_cost_usd. Halted flag flips true when the running
1126	# total meets or exceeds the cap.
1127	append_budget_cell() {
1128	  local out="$1" cell_id="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4" cost="$5"
1129	  local bp tmp
1130	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1131	  tmp="$(mktemp)"
1132	  jq \
1133	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1134	    --arg task "${task}" \
1135	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1136	    --argjson cost "${cost}" \
1137	    '
1138	      .cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, cost_usd:$cost}]
1139	      | .total_cost_usd = ((.total_cost_usd // 0) + $cost)
1140	      | .halted = ((.total_cost_usd >= (.max_cost_usd // 0))
1141	                   and ((.max_cost_usd // 0) > 0))
1142	    ' "${bp}" >"${tmp}"
1143	  mv "${tmp}" "${bp}"
1144	  jq -r '.total_cost_usd' "${bp}"
1145	}

1147	# budget_is_halted exits 0 when the budget cap has been hit (halted=true in
1148	# budget.json). Used by run_sweep to gate cells before invoking the executor.
1149	budget_is_halted() {
1150	  local bp
1151	  bp="$(budget_path "$1")"
1152	  [[ -f "${bp}" ]] || return 1
1153	  local halted

1158	# write_budget_halted_cell writes a placeholder cell that did not execute

1161	write_budget_halted_cell() {
1162	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1163	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1164	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" cap="${10}"
1165	  local te_ver="${11}" hd="${12}"

1172	  : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"

1176	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"

1185	  jq -n \
1186	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \

1191	    --arg now "${now}" \

1196	    --argjson cost 0 \

1198	       task_id:$task_id,
1199	       framework:$framework,
1200	       dataset:$dataset,
1201	       cell_id:$cell_id,
1202	       started_at:$now,
1203	       finished_at:$now,
1204	       rep:$rep,
1205	       profile:$profile,
1206	       command:[],

1209	       result:{},
1210	       final_status:"budget_halted",
1211	       invalid_class:"",
1212	       process_outcome:"setup_failed",

1214	       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,

1222	# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,
1223	# task-spec composition, task-executor invocation with retry, report.json
1224	# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into
1225	# the prior (attempt_of) cell. Registers as in-flight at start, unregisters at end.
1226	run_one_cell() {
1227	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1228	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1229	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" harness_adapter="${10}"
1230	  local attempt_of="${11}"
1231	  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
1232	  local interrupted_flag="${14:-}"
1233	
1234	  local cell_id cell_dir
1235	  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
1236	  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
1237	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"
1238	
1239	  # Handler for SIGTERM during cell execution
1240	  write_interrupted_report() {
1241	    local now
1242	    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1243	    jq -n \
1244	      --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1245	      --arg task "${task}" \
1246	      --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1247	      --arg now "${now}" \
1248	      --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1249	      --arg hd "${hd}" \
1250	      '{
1251	         cell_id:$cell_id,
1252	         task_id:$task,
1253	         framework:"terminal-bench",
1254	         dataset:"terminal-bench-2-1",
1255	         started_at:$now,
1256	         finished_at:$now,
1257	         rep:"1/1",
1258	         final_status:"interrupted",
1259	         invalid_class:"",
1260	         process_outcome:"killed",
1261	         cost_usd_at_run_time:0,
1262	         task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1263	         harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
1264	       }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1265	    mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1266	  }
1267	
1268	  # Register as in-flight so signal handlers can interrupt us
1269	  register_inflight "${cell_id}" "${cell_dir}"
1270	  trap 'write_interrupted_report; unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM; exit 143' TERM
1271	  trap 'unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM' RETURN
1272	
1273	  # Check if global interrupt flag is set before starting
1274	  if [[ -n "${interrupted_flag}" && -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]]; then
1275	    warn "cell ${cell_id}: halting due to signal interrupt"
1276	    # Write interrupted report immediately
1277	    write_interrupted_report
1278	    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1279	    return 0
1280	  fi
1281	
1282	  local started_at
1283	  started_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1284	  jq -n \
1285	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1286	    --arg task "${task}" \
1287	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1288	    --arg started "${started_at}" \
1289	    '{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, started_at:$started, status:"running"}' \
1290	    >"${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
1291	
1292	  local profile_json
1293	  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
1294	
1295	  local adapter_path="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}/${harness_adapter}"
1296	  [[ -x "${adapter_path}" ]] || die "harness-adapter missing/not executable: ${adapter_path}"
1297	
1298	  local command_spec install_spec harbor_plugin
1299	  install_spec="$("${adapter_path}" install "/installed-agent/${harness_adapter}")" \
1300	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} install failed"
1301	  harbor_plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"${install_spec}")"
1302	  command_spec="$(printf '%s' "${profile_json}" | "${adapter_path}" command)" \
1303	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} command failed"
1304	
1305	  local env_json secret_keys_json command_json env_redacted
1306	  env_json="$(jq -c '.env // {}' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1307	  secret_keys_json="$(jq -c '.secret_env_keys // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1308	  command_json="$(jq -c '.command // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1309	  env_redacted="$(compose_env_redacted "${env_json}" "${secret_keys_json}")"
1310	
1311	  local tasks_dir
1312	  tasks_dir="$(resolve_tasks_dir "${framework}")"
1313	
1314	  local task_spec
1315	  task_spec="$(jq -nc \
1316	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
1317	    --arg tasks_dir "${tasks_dir}" \
1318	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
1319	    --arg plugin "${harbor_plugin}" \
1320	    --arg image "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" \
1321	    --argjson env "${env_json}" \
1322	    --argjson secret_env_keys "${secret_keys_json}" \
1323	    --arg job_name "${cell_id}" \
1324	    '{task_id:$task_id, tasks_dir:$tasks_dir, cell_dir:$cell_dir,
1325	      harbor_plugin:$plugin, image:$image, env:$env,
1326	      secret_env_keys:$secret_env_keys,
1327	      extra_args:["--jobs-dir","/output","--job-name",$job_name]}')"
1328	
1329	  local executor="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
1330	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
1331	    executor="${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE}"
1332	  fi
1333	  [[ -x "${executor}" ]] || die "task-executor missing/not executable: ${executor}"
1334	
1335	  local exec_status=0
1336	  run_executor_with_retry "${task_spec}" "${cell_dir}" "${executor}" || exec_status=$?
1337	
1338	  local finished_at
1339	  finished_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1340	
1341	  local result_json="{}"
1342	  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]]; then
1343	    if jq -e '.' "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
1344	      result_json="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
1345	    fi
1346	  fi
1347	
1348	  local final_status invalid_class
1349	  if [[ ${exec_status} -eq 0 ]]; then
1350	    final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // .status // "completed"' <<<"${result_json}")"
1351	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' <<<"${result_json}")"
1352	  else
1353	    final_status="transient_exhausted"
1354	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // "transient_exhausted"' <<<"${result_json}")"
1355	  fi
1356	
1357	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
1358	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"
1359	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"
1360	
1361	  local rep_str
1362	  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
1363	
1364	  local cost_usd
1365	  cost_usd="$(compute_cell_cost "${result_json}" "${profile_json}")"
1366	  [[ -n "${cost_usd}" ]] || cost_usd="0"
1367	
1368	  local report
1369	  report="$(jq -n \
1370	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1371	    --argjson command "${command_json}" \
1372	    --argjson env_redacted "${env_redacted}" \
1373	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1374	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
1375	    --arg framework "${framework}" \
1376	    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
1377	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1378	    --arg started_at "${started_at}" \
1379	    --arg finished_at "${finished_at}" \
1380	    --arg final_status "${final_status}" \
1381	    --arg invalid_class "${invalid_class}" \
1382	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1383	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
1384	    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
1385	    --arg attempt_of "${attempt_of}" \
1386	    --argjson cost "${cost_usd}" \
1387	    '{
1388	       task_id:$task_id,
1389	       framework:$framework,
1390	       dataset:$dataset,
1391	       cell_id:$cell_id,
1392	       started_at:$started_at,
1393	       finished_at:$finished_at,
1394	       rep:$rep,
1395	       profile:$profile,
1396	       command:$command,
1397	       env_redacted:$env_redacted,
1398	       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
1399	       result:$result,
1400	       final_status:$final_status,
1401	       invalid_class:$invalid_class,
1402	       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
1403	       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1404	       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
1405	     } | if $attempt_of != "" then . + {attempt_of:$attempt_of} else . end')"
1406	
1407	  printf '%s\n' "${report}" >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1408	  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1409	
1410	  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
1411	
1412	  if [[ -n "${attempt_of}" && -f "${attempt_of}/report.json" ]]; then
1413	    local tmp
1414	    tmp="$(mktemp)"
1415	    jq --arg sup "${cell_id}" '. + {superseded_by:$sup}' \
1416	      "${attempt_of}/report.json" >"${tmp}"
1417	    mv "${tmp}" "${attempt_of}/report.json"
1418	  elif [[ -n "${attempt_of}" ]]; then
1419	    # Orphan prior cell with no report.json: write a minimal stub so the
1420	    # superseded_by back-link is recorded.
1421	    jq -n \
1422	      --arg sup "${cell_id}" \
1423	      --arg orphan_of "$(basename "${attempt_of}")" \
1424	      '{final_status:"orphan", invalid_class:"orphan_cell_state", cell_id:$orphan_of, superseded_by:$sup}' \
1425	      >"${attempt_of}/report.json"
1426	    rm -f "${attempt_of}/cell-state.json"
1427	  fi
1428	
1429	  # Unregister from in-flight (trap will also call this on return)
1430	  unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"
1431	  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1432	}
1433	
1434	# budget_or_run_cell either writes a budget-halted placeholder (when the
1435	# budget cap has been hit) or invokes run_one_cell. On success it appends the
1436	# cell's cost into <out>/budget.json so the halt flag flips for the next
1437	# cell. cap="" disables budget machinery entirely. interrupted_flag="" disables
1438	# interrupt checking.
1439	budget_or_run_cell() {
1440	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1441	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1442	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}"

1501	    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
1502	    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
1503	    touch "${interrupted_flag}"

1506	    interrupt_inflight_cells

1509	    local pids
1510	    pids="$(jobs -p)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner_signals.sh - Tests for SIGTERM/SIGINT handling in the benchmark runner
#
# Verifies:
# - SIGTERM stops cell submission and marks in-flight cells as interrupted
# - Docker containers are cleaned up on signal
# - Runner exits non-zero on signal
# - SIGINT has the same behavior as SIGTERM

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"
BENCH_RESULTS_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/test-results"

# Helper to clean up test artifacts
cleanup_test() {
  rm -rf "${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/signal-test-$$"
}

trap cleanup_test EXIT

# Test SIGTERM: send SIGTERM after runner starts, verify interrupted status
test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers() {
  local test_name="sigterm"
  local out="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/signal-test-$$/${test_name}"
  mkdir -p "${out}"

  echo "Running SIGTERM test..."

  # Start the benchmark in background
  timeout 15 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --out "${out}" &
  local runner_pid=$!

  # Give the runner time to start executing the first cell
  sleep 5

  # Send SIGTERM
  kill -TERM "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Wait for the process to exit
  local exit_code=0
  wait "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || exit_code=$?

  # Verify exit code is non-zero
  if [[ ${exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "FAIL: SIGTERM test - runner exited with code 0, expected non-zero"
    return 1
  fi

  # Check that there are cells with interrupted status
  local interrupted_count=0
  if [[ -d "${out}/cells" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r report; do
      if jq -e '.final_status == "interrupted"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        interrupted_count=$((interrupted_count + 1))
        # Verify process_outcome is killed
        if ! jq -e '.process_outcome == "killed"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "FAIL: SIGTERM test - process_outcome is not 'killed' in ${report}"
          return 1
        fi
      fi
    done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" 2>/dev/null)
  fi

  # Verify at least one cell was interrupted
  if [[ ${interrupted_count} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARN: SIGTERM test - no interrupted cells found (may be normal if no cells started)"
  fi

  # Verify no docker containers are running for this runner
  # (This is a soft check as containers may have already exited)
  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    local running_containers
    running_containers=$(docker ps -q --filter "ancestor=fizeau-harbor-runner" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    if [[ ${running_containers} -gt 0 ]]; then
      echo "WARN: SIGTERM test - found ${running_containers} running harbor-runner containers"
      docker ps --filter "ancestor=fizeau-harbor-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi

  echo "PASS: SIGTERM test"
  return 0
}

# Test SIGINT: same as SIGTERM but using SIGINT
test_sigint_same_behavior() {
  local test_name="sigint"
  local out="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/signal-test-$$/${test_name}"
  mkdir -p "${out}"

  echo "Running SIGINT test..."

  # Start the benchmark in background
  timeout 15 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --out "${out}" &
  local runner_pid=$!

  # Give the runner time to start executing the first cell
  sleep 5

  # Send SIGINT
  kill -INT "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Wait for the process to exit
  local exit_code=0
  wait "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || exit_code=$?

  # Verify exit code is non-zero
  if [[ ${exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "FAIL: SIGINT test - runner exited with code 0, expected non-zero"
    return 1
  fi

  # Check that there are cells with interrupted status
  local interrupted_count=0
  if [[ -d "${out}/cells" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r report; do
      if jq -e '.final_status == "interrupted"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        interrupted_count=$((interrupted_count + 1))
        # Verify process_outcome is killed
        if ! jq -e '.process_outcome == "killed"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "FAIL: SIGINT test - process_outcome is not 'killed' in ${report}"
          return 1
        fi
      fi
    done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" 2>/dev/null)
  fi

  # Verify at least one cell was interrupted
  if [[ ${interrupted_count} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARN: SIGINT test - no interrupted cells found (may be normal if no cells started)"
  fi

  echo "PASS: SIGINT test"
  return 0
}

# Main test runner
main() {

  if [[ ${failed} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "FAILED: ${failed} tests failed"
    return 1
  fi
  echo "SUCCESS: All signal tests passed"

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner_concurrency.sh - Tests for concurrency group locking and in-flight tracking
#
# Verifies:
# - Concurrency group flock serializes runs on the same group
# - in-flight.json tracks cells and prunes dead PIDs
# - Multiple profiles with the same concurrency group serialize properly

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"
BENCH_RESULTS_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/test-results"
STATE_DIR="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark"

# Helper to clean up test artifacts
cleanup_test() {
  rm -rf "${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/concurrency-test-$$"
}

trap cleanup_test EXIT

# Test that concurrency group flock serializes runs
test_concurrency_group_flock_serializes() {
  local test_name="flock-serialize"
  local out1="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/concurrency-test-$$/${test_name}-1"
  local out2="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/concurrency-test-$$/${test_name}-2"
  mkdir -p "${out1}" "${out2}"

  echo "Running concurrency group flock serialization test..."

  # Start first benchmark with the same concurrency group
  timeout 30 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out1}" &
  local pid1=$!

  # Small delay to ensure first is well underway
  sleep 2

  # Start second benchmark with the same group (should wait for first to complete)
  local time2_start
  time2_start="$(date +%s)"

  timeout 30 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out2}" &
  local pid2=$!

  # Wait for both to complete
  wait "${pid1}" 2>/dev/null || true
  wait "${pid2}" 2>/dev/null || true

  local time2_end
  time2_end="$(date +%s)"

  # Verify both completed with results
  if [[ -f "${out1}/cells" ]]; then
    local count1
    count1=$(find "${out1}/cells" -name "report.json" | wc -l)
    if [[ ${count1} -lt 1 ]]; then
      echo "FAIL: concurrency test - no cells completed in first runner"
      return 1
    fi
  fi

  if [[ -f "${out2}/cells" ]]; then
    local count2
    count2=$(find "${out2}/cells" -name "report.json" | wc -l)
    if [[ ${count2} -lt 1 ]]; then
      echo "WARN: concurrency test - no cells completed in second runner (may be normal)"
    fi
  fi

  echo "PASS: concurrency group flock serialization test"
  return 0
}

# Test that in-flight.json tracks cells correctly
test_in_flight_json_tracks_cells() {
  local test_name="inflight-tracking"
  local out="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/concurrency-test-$$/${test_name}"
  mkdir -p "${out}"

  echo "Running in-flight.json tracking test..."

  # Run a benchmark and check in-flight.json during execution
  timeout 20 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
  local pid=$!

  # Give it time to start a cell
  sleep 3

  # Check if in-flight.json exists and is valid JSON
  local hostname
  hostname="$(hostname)"
  local inflight_json="${STATE_DIR}/${hostname}/in-flight.json"

  if [[ -f "${inflight_json}" ]]; then
    if ! jq -e '.cells | type == "array"' "${inflight_json}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "FAIL: in-flight.json is not valid JSON"
      return 1
    fi
    echo "in-flight.json found and valid"
  fi

  # Wait for benchmark to complete
  wait "${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Check that in-flight.json was cleaned up (no cells should be listed as running)
  # The file may exist but cells array should be empty or have only stale entries
  if [[ -f "${inflight_json}" ]]; then
    local cell_count
    cell_count=$(jq '.cells | length' "${inflight_json}" 2>/dev/null || printf '0')
    if [[ ${cell_count} -gt 0 ]]; then
      # Try to prune dead PIDs and recheck
      # This simulates what the runner would do on the next invocation
      local should_be_empty=1
      while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
        [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
        local cell_pid
        cell_pid="$(printf '%s' "${cell_json}" | jq -r '.pid')"
        if kill -0 "${cell_pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
          should_be_empty=0
        fi
      done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${inflight_json}")

      if [[ ${should_be_empty} -eq 1 ]]; then
        echo "in-flight.json cleanup verified (stale PIDs present but not running)"
      fi
    fi
  fi

  echo "PASS: in-flight.json tracking test"
  return 0
}

# Main test runner
main() {
  mkdir -p "${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}"

  local failed=0
  test_concurrency_group_flock_serializes || failed=$((failed + 1))
  test_in_flight_json_tracks_cells || failed=$((failed + 1))

  if [[ ${failed} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "FAILED: ${failed} tests failed"
    return 1
  fi
  echo "SUCCESS: All concurrency tests passed"
  return 0
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner_budget.sh - Tests for budget enforcement
#
# Verifies:
# - --max-cost-usd halts execution when budget cap is reached
# - Halted cells have final_status=budget_halted and process_outcome=setup_failed
# - budget.json accumulates costs correctly
# - budget.json total_usd matches sum of by_cell costs

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"
BENCH_RESULTS_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/test-results"

# Helper to clean up test artifacts
cleanup_test() {
  rm -rf "${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/budget-test-$$"
}

trap cleanup_test EXIT

# Test that max-cost-usd produces budget_halted cells
test_max_cost_usd_produces_budget_halted() {
  local test_name="budget-halt"
  local out="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/budget-test-$$/${test_name}"
  mkdir -p "${out}"

  echo "Running max-cost-usd halt test..."

  # Run with a very low cost cap to force budget halt
  # Using 0.01 USD which should be reached quickly
  timeout 30 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --max-cost-usd 0.01 \
    --out "${out}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

  # Check that at least one cell has budget_halted status
  local halted_count=0
  local total_cost=0
  if [[ -d "${out}/cells" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r report; do
      if jq -e '.final_status == "budget_halted"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        halted_count=$((halted_count + 1))
        # Verify process_outcome is setup_failed
        if ! jq -e '.process_outcome == "setup_failed"' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "FAIL: budget halt test - process_outcome is not 'setup_failed' in ${report}"
          return 1
        fi
        # Verify note field is populated
        if ! jq -e '.note | length > 0' "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "FAIL: budget halt test - note field missing in ${report}"
          return 1
        fi
      fi
      # Accumulate costs
      local cell_cost
      cell_cost=$(jq -r '.cost_usd_at_run_time // 0' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || printf '0')
      total_cost=$(printf '%f + %f\n' "${total_cost}" "${cell_cost}" | bc 2>/dev/null || printf '0')
    done < <(find "${out}/cells" -name "report.json" 2>/dev/null)
  fi

  # Verify at least one cell was halted (if any cells ran)
  if [[ ${halted_count} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARN: budget halt test - no budget_halted cells found (may be normal if budget was large)"
  else
    echo "Found ${halted_count} budget-halted cells"
  fi

  # Verify total cost doesn't exceed budget
  if (( $(printf '%f' "${total_cost}") > 0.02 )); then
    echo "WARN: budget halt test - total cost ${total_cost} exceeds expected cap + margin"
  fi

  echo "PASS: max-cost-usd halt test"
  return 0
}

# Test that budget.json accumulates correctly
test_budget_json_accumulates() {
  local test_name="budget-accumulate"
  local out="${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}/budget-test-$$/${test_name}"
  mkdir -p "${out}"

  echo "Running budget.json accumulation test..."

  # Run with a moderate budget
  timeout 30 "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile sindri-lucebox \
    --bench-set tb-2-1-canary \
    --jobs 1 \
    --max-cost-usd 100.0 \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${out}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

  # Verify budget.json exists and has correct structure
  if [[ ! -f "${out}/budget.json" ]]; then
    echo "FAIL: budget.json not created"
    return 1
  fi

  # Validate budget.json structure
  if ! jq -e '
    type == "object"
    and (.max_cost_usd | type == "number")
    and (.total_cost_usd | type == "number")
    and (.halted | type == "boolean")
    and (.cells | type == "array")
    and all(.cells[]?;
      type == "object"
      and (.cell_id | type == "string")
      and (.task | type == "string")
      and (.profile | type == "string")
      and (.cost_usd | type == "number" or .cost_usd == null)
    )
  ' "${out}/budget.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "FAIL: budget.json structure is invalid"
    jq '.' "${out}/budget.json" || true
    return 1
  fi

  # Verify total_cost_usd equals sum of cell costs
  local total_from_header
  total_from_header=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "${out}/budget.json")

  local sum_from_cells
  sum_from_cells=$(jq '[.cells[] | .cost_usd // 0] | add // 0' "${out}/budget.json")

  # Compare with tolerance for floating point
  local diff
  diff=$(printf '%f - %f' "${total_from_header}" "${sum_from_cells}" | bc 2>/dev/null || printf '0')
  diff="${diff#-}"  # Remove negative sign

  if (( $(printf '%f' "${diff}") > 0.0001 )); then
    echo "FAIL: budget.json total_cost_usd (${total_from_header}) != sum of cells (${sum_from_cells})"
    return 1
  fi

  echo "budget.json validates: total=${total_from_header}, cells_sum=${sum_from_cells}"
  echo "PASS: budget.json accumulation test"
  return 0
}

# Main test runner
main() {
  mkdir -p "${BENCH_RESULTS_DIR}"

  local failed=0
  test_max_cost_usd_produces_budget_halted || failed=$((failed + 1))
  test_budget_json_accumulates || failed=$((failed + 1))

  if [[ ${failed} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "FAILED: ${failed} tests failed"
    return 1
  fi
  echo "SUCCESS: All budget tests passed"
  return 0
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/benchmark/test/run.sh - Master test runner for benchmark system tests
#
# Runs all integration tests for:
# - Signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT)
# - Concurrency group locking
# - In-flight.json tracking
# - Budget enforcement
# - cmd/bench redirects

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEST_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/test"

# Color codes for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Global test result tracking
FAILED_TESTS=()
PASSED_TESTS=()

# Helper to run a test and track results
run_test() {
  local test_name="$1"
  local test_file="$2"

  echo -e "\n${YELLOW}=== Running ${test_name} ===${NC}"

  if [[ ! -f "${test_file}" ]]; then
    echo -e "${RED}ERROR: Test file not found: ${test_file}${NC}"
    FAILED_TESTS+=("${test_name}")
    return 1
  fi

  if bash "${test_file}"; then
    PASSED_TESTS+=("${test_name}")
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ ${test_name} passed${NC}"
    return 0
  else
    FAILED_TESTS+=("${test_name}")
    echo -e "${RED}✗ ${test_name} failed${NC}"
    return 1
  fi
}

# Test AC1: cmd/bench redirects
test_bench_redirects() {
  echo -e "\n${YELLOW}=== Testing cmd/bench redirects (AC1, AC8) ===${NC}"

  # Verify go test passes
  if ! go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestBenchMainRedirectsExecutionSubcommands >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo -e "${RED}✗ TestBenchMainRedirectsExecutionSubcommands failed${NC}"
    FAILED_TESTS+=("cmd/bench-redirects")
    return 1
  fi
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ TestBenchMainRedirectsExecutionSubcommands passed${NC}"

  # Verify fiz-bench command redirects
  if ! fiz-bench matrix 2>&1 | grep -q 'use ./benchmark'; then
    echo -e "${RED}✗ fiz-bench matrix does not redirect to ./benchmark${NC}"
    FAILED_TESTS+=("fiz-bench-redirect")
    return 1
  fi
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ fiz-bench matrix redirects correctly${NC}"

  PASSED_TESTS+=("cmd/bench-redirects")
  return 0
}

main() {
  echo "Starting benchmark system tests..."

  # Change to repo root
  cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" || exit 1

  # Run unit tests for cmd/bench
  echo -e "\n${YELLOW}=== Go unit tests ===${NC}"
  if go test ./cmd/bench/... >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PASSED_TESTS+=("go-unit-tests")
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Go unit tests passed${NC}"
  else
    FAILED_TESTS+=("go-unit-tests")
    echo -e "${RED}✗ Go unit tests failed${NC}"
  fi

  # Run cmd/bench redirect tests
  test_bench_redirects || true

  # Run integration tests (bash-based)
  # These are currently disabled by default as they require:
  # - Docker to be running
  # - Harbor image to be available
  # - Actual benchmark infrastructure to be in place
  #
  # Uncomment to run them when environment is ready:
  #run_test "signal-handling" "${TEST_DIR}/test_runner_signals.sh" || true
  #run_test "concurrency-locking" "${TEST_DIR}/test_runner_concurrency.sh" || true
  #run_test "budget-enforcement" "${TEST_DIR}/test_runner_budget.sh" || true

  # Print summary
  echo -e "\n${YELLOW}=== Test Summary ===${NC}"
  echo "Passed: ${#PASSED_TESTS[@]}"
  for test in "${PASSED_TESTS[@]}"; do
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} ${test}"
  done

  echo "Failed: ${#FAILED_TESTS[@]}"
  for test in "${FAILED_TESTS[@]}"; do
    echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} ${test}"
  done

  if [[ ${#FAILED_TESTS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo -e "\n${RED}Some tests failed${NC}"
    return 1
  else
    echo -e "\n${GREEN}All tests passed${NC}"
    return 0
  fi
}

main "$@"
